name: A167 DURHAM WESTERN BY PASS 15A
units: metres, transcribed verbatim from a typed Wimpey Geotech site-investigation log; depth = base of interval below ground level

annotations: cable percussion overburden 0.00-2.00m, no samples recovered; lithology not logged

intervals.verbatim: Firm mottled dark brown and grey sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse subangular and subrounded gravel
annotations: irregular (chevron) upper contact drawn at 2.00m; U(100) 35 blows at 2.00m

intervals.verbatim: Dark brown occasionally light brown clayey silty fine to coarse SAND with some fine to medium subrounded gravel and black coal dust laminae
Firm dark brown laminated sandy silty CLAY with some thin bands of light and dark brown fine to coarse sand and occasional fine to coarse subangular and subrounded gravel
Medium dense dark brown clayey occasionally slightly clayey becoming very clayey silty fine to medium becoming fine to coarse SAND with occasional fine to medium subangular and subrounded gravel
annotations: groundwater encountered (GWe) at 4.40m, rose to 4.00m in 30 minutes; seepage cut off by casing (GWc) at 7.00m; S20 / Db samples

intervals.verbatim: Light greyish brown slightly silty fine to coarse SAND and fine to coarse subangular GRAVEL
annotations: borehole advanced by chiselling 9.30-9.80m (1h); U(100) 130 blows at 8.80m; DRY at base of cable percussion; end of cable percussion at 9.90m

intervals.verbatim: Yellow fine grained very highly to completely weathered broken silty MUDSTONE - weak
annotations: start of rotary core drilling; core run TCR 89 / SCR 7 / RQD (0)

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey fine grained moderately weathered extremely thinly irregularly bedded highly fractured silty MUDSTONE - weak and moderately weak
annotations: core runs TCR/SCR/(RQD): 0/0/(0), 70/0/(0), 100/6/(0); fracture index >20; casing to 12.50m

intervals.verbatim: Grey fine grained slightly to moderately weathered thinly planar laminated slightly fissile predominantly highly fractured muddy SILTSTONE - moderately weak to moderately strong. Extremely closely spaced sandstone laminae and subhorizontal bedding fractures. Some subvertical ironstained fractures
annotations: core run TCR 100 / SCR 18 / RQD (6); fracture index >20

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey fine grained slightly and slightly to moderately weathered thinly planar laminated fissile silty MUDSTONE - moderately strong and weak. Very closely and extremely closely spaced subhorizontal bedding fractures. Common subvertical fractures. Core very highly shattered from 17.60m to 17.90m
annotations: core run TCR 100 / SCR 11 / RQD (0); fracture index >20; core very highly shattered 17.60-17.90m

intervals.verbatim: Very dark grey and dark grey fine grained moderately and slightly weathered thinly planar and ripple laminated sandy micaceous SILTSTONE - moderately weak and moderately strong. Moderately weathered darker bands are carbonaceous. Occasional orange sandstone bands at top. Closely and very closely spaced subhorizontal weathered bedding fractures and common subvertical fractures. Occasional highly fractured bands
annotations: core run TCR 100 / SCR 54 / RQD (12); fracture indices 13 to >20; carbonaceous darker bands

intervals.verbatim: Very dark grey and dark grey locally discoloured brown moderately locally moderately to highly weathered thinly planar and ripple laminated sandy micaceous often carbonaceous SILTSTONE - moderately weak and moderately strong to strong. Core predominantly very highly fractured with some competent beds up to 0.20m thick. Occasional completely weathered very weak bands
annotations: core runs TCR/SCR/(RQD): 100/20/(0), 100/23/(4); fracture index >20; carbonaceous

intervals.verbatim: Grey fine grained slightly weathered thinly laminated slightly disturbed very silty SANDSTONE - strong occasionally moderately strong. Closely and very closely spaced subhorizontal slightly weathered bedding fractures. Common thin carbonaceous siltstone becoming mudstone from 27.70m to 28.10m. Dark grey moderately weak mudstone from 29.20m to 29.50m
annotations: core runs TCR/SCR/(RQD): 100/71/(32), 100/37/(9); fracture indices 11-13 and >20; carbonaceous siltstone/mudstone band 27.70-28.10m; mudstone band 29.20-29.50m

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey fine grained moderately discoloured orangeish brown moderately weathered thinly ripple laminated predominantly very highly fractured sandy SILTSTONE - moderately strong and strong rock fragments. Extremely closely spaced subhorizontal and subvertical ironstained fractures
annotations: core run TCR 100 / SCR 12 / RQD (18); fracture index >20; ironstained fractures

intervals.verbatim: Yellow fine grained highly discoloured moderately weathered SANDSTONE - strong
annotations: fracture index 8; DRY; END OF BOREHOLE at 35.30m

notes: Typed Wimpey Geotech (WIMPEY GEOTECH) site-investigation log, Fig. 17, 4 sheets; A167 Durham Western Bypass, Borehole 15A. Cable percussion to 9.90m then rotary core drilling to 35.30m final depth (matches BGS index 35.3m). Ground level 112.45 m OD; every OD Level printed in the log equals 112.45 minus the stated depth, so the OD column was used to anchor all 15 strata boundaries and no clerk arithmetic slips were found. Location box gives a local site grid (5740.2E/4085.2N), distinct from the National Grid header (425736,544079) used above. Sequence: ~9.9m of drift (no-recovery topsoil, glacial clays/sands/gravels) over weathered Coal Measures bedrock (interbedded mudstone / siltstone / sandstone). Intermediate depth marks 14.00, 15.85, 23.55, 26.55, 29.60 and 32.30 carry no OD and coincide with TCR-block and fracture-index (If) divisions, so they are core-run boundaries, not strata boundaries. No COAL beds logged and no pencil marginalia / seam correlations present, hence no seam_marks. Remarks (sheet 4): advanced by chiselling 9.30-9.80m (1h); groundwater encountered at 4.40m, level rose to 4.00m in 30 min; seepage cut off by casing at 7.00m; full return of circulation air throughout rotary drilling; backfilled with bentonite/cement grout. Interval 1 ('No samples') classed unknown as the top 2m lithology was never logged.
